title: rubus fruticosus subsp. rhamnifolius
intro: Rubus fruticosus subsp. rhamnifolius, also known as Himalayan raspberry or Himalayan blackberry, is a subspecies of the blackberry plant (Rubus fruticosus) native to the Himalayan region and parts of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its high antioxidant and anti-inflammatory content. The health benefits of Rubus fruticosus subsp. rhamnifolius include its ability to reduce blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and act as an antimicrobial agent. The plant is commonly prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or dried extract, and is also used in jams and preserves due to its sweet and tangy flavor.
